briefing.headline: Rig up a Drop Shot — finesse spawn approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Drop Shot is a strong choice for spawn with stained water and post frontal conditions.

Johnston: On deep Lake Ontario smallmouth at 33 feet, use a drop shot with a 3/8-ounce weight in all conditions. On calm days, cast it out and let it sit — don't move it. On rough, windy days, cast past the fish and drag it through them.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

primaryApproach.targets: Focus on flat, grass, dock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Johnston: Use a heavier drop shot weight up-current from structure to keep the bait down in current. In slacker water behind the structure, use a lighter weight for the same presentation, which is imp
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on flat, grass, dock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Johnston: Use Garmin Perspective Mode when seeing bed fish in low-visibility conditions. This was one of two main tools (along with flipping and drop shot baits) for bed fishing at the St. Johns River.
Yamamoto pro Fukae: Uses 14-lb YGK sinking braid to an 8-lb fluorocarbon leader for spawn-time drop-shot and Ned Rig presentations on a Shimano Expride 170M with 2500-size spinning reel

KVD: 'Don't assume you must finesse spawning bass. At the 2011 Classic, 4 of top 7 finishers power fished spinnerbaits and crankbaits on the same 150-yard spawning flat. Key conditions: hard bottom flat with stumps + grass, 10-degree water temp ri...
Wheeler: Cherokee smallmouth were feeding up, not down — a dropshot dropped in front of them wouldn't get bit. Had to keep the bait 2 ft above their heads for them to come up off the bottom and eat

briefing.depthStrategy: Post-frontal spawning bass become extremely lockjaw but won't abandon beds. Fish are still in 2-5ft but much harder to trigger. Slow way down and make repeated presentations to visible beds.
adjustIf: If skies cloud up and wind picks up, re-introduce moving baits — the reprieve from high pressure activates fish
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If you get followers without commits, add scent or downsize your hook/weight
